---
title: "Procedural Memory and Cognitive Loop Closure"
chapter_id: "procedural-memory-and-cognitive-loop-closure"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "cognitive_loop_closure"
  - "rmi"
  - "rgs"
  - "benchmaxxing"
  - "talos"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Procedural Memory and Cognitive Loop Closure

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `procedural-memory-and-cognitive-loop-closure` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `cognitive_loop_closure`, `rmi`, `rgs`, `benchmaxxing`, `talos`, `moecot` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Repeated reasoning trajectories should not be improvised forever when they can become verified procedures or tools.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Agents waste cognition and introduce inconsistency when recurring workflows are repeatedly reconstructed from scratch.

## Core Claim

[procedural-memory-and-cognitive-loop-closure.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Cognitive loop closure compiles repeated reasoning into verified parameterized tools and procedural memory.

## Mechanism

- Log safe execution traces.
- Detect recurring loops.
- Abstract invariant structure, discover parameters, verify, and publish tools.

## Interfaces

- Artifact graph supplies traces.
- Routing selects new tools.
- Evidence tests utility and regressions.

## Invariants

- Only verified loops become tools.
- Parameters and assumptions are explicit.
- Tool promotion records residuals and regressions.

## Failure modes

- Premature toolification.
- Hidden assumptions.
- Procedural memory poisoning.

## Minimal implementation

A loop closure pipeline from trace detection to verified tool record.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Loop detection test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Tool abstraction test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Verified tool regression test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `cognitive_loop_closure` | Cognitive Loop Closure | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Repeated cognition should become procedural memory / verified tools. |
| `rmi` | Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmark pressure, residual escrow, verified modular capability, regression preservation. |
| `rgs` | Ratcheting Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Bridge between active compression, procedural memory, benchmark frontiers, verified AI growth. |
| `benchmaxxing` | Benchmaxxing: The Performance Ratchet | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmarks as pressure surfaces, saturation -> regression, harder frontier, anti-Goodhart safeguards. |
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
